QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_7

question.text: Mr. Deepak incurred the following losses in FY 2025-26: Non-speculative business loss Rs.5,00,000 and Short-term capital loss Rs.2,00,000. His salary income is Rs.12,00,000. Which of the following is the correct treatment?

answer.text: Both losses can be set off against salary income, reducing taxable income to Rs.5,00,000
Non-speculative business loss Rs.5,00,000 can be set off against salary; STCL of Rs.2,00,000 is carried forward
Neither loss can be set off against salary — non-speculative business loss cannot be set off against salary, and capital loss cannot be set off against any other head; both are carried forward
STCL Rs.2,00,000 can be set off against salary; business loss of Rs.5,00,000 is carried forward

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Non-speculative business loss can be set off against any income EXCEPT salary. Capital loss (both STCL and LTCL) CANNOT be set off against any other head of income — only against capital gains. Since Deepak's only other income is salary, neither loss can be set off. Both are carried forward: business loss for 8 years (against business income only) and STCL for 8 years (against any capital gain). **The Trap:** Option B is very tempting — many candidates wrongly assume "any income except salary" means inter-head set-off is possible with salary. The rule is the opposite: business loss can be set off against other heads EXCEPT salary. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A tests basic set-off rules. The salary exception for business loss is a frequently tested distinction. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Salaried clients with side businesses should understand that business losses won't reduce their salary tax liability. They need other non-salary income to absorb the loss.
